concerns.analysis: On Page 15, the output contains a URL in the footer that does not match the source PDF. The source URL points to a specific SharePoint/ShareDrive path ending in an Excel file '[Tables and Figures.xlsx]Figure 1'. The output hallucinates an entirely different subdomain ('projectweb'), path ('CWContent', '112', 'DF.002'), and file type ('Tables and Figures.aprx').
concerns.source_pdf_quote: https://projectsitesb.geosyntec.com/5/FWConsentOrder/Shared Documents/12 - Outfall 002 Reductions/2020 Misc Tasks/OF 002 Mass Loading/Tables and Figures/[Tables and Figures.xlsx]Figure 1
concerns.output_quote: https://projectweb.geosyntec.com/CWContent/Shared Documents/112 - Outfall 002 Reductions/2020 Misc Tasks/DF.002 Mass Loading/Tables and Figures/Tables and Figures.aprx | Figure 1

concerns.analysis: On page 15 of the source document, there is a large, highly meaningful aerial map illustrating the Outfall 002 Sampling Locations (Figure 1). This image is entirely missing from the HTML output; only the small scale bar is present as an image. Additionally, on page 16, the flowchart for Figure 2 is missing its image element entirely and instead displays a text placeholder `[Image: A flowchart detailing the logic...]`.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Large aerial map showing 'Outfall 002 Sampling Locations' (Figure 1)
concerns.output_quote: The HTML lacks an element for the map, showing only the scale bar image and text/tables for the legend.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (43): DocuSign Envelope ID: 1B477D8C-F5AD-4BCC-B766-EF28605DC046; Geosyntec Consultants of NC, P.C.
NC License No.: C-3500 and; Outfall 002 Mass Load
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=43, pages_affected=15/17. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 2 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis:
caption_as_header=2. Examples: p15: "Outfall 002 Sampling Locations
Chemours Fayetteville Works, "; p17: "Flowchart for Determining the Concentration for Loading at t". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "- HFPO-DA and PFHpA are also analyzed by EPA Metho". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description:
Repeated footer/header text (2x): "geosyntec consultants of nc, p.c.
nc license no.: c-3500 and"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=2. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
